page: Vultr vbm-24c-384gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring an AMD EPYC 9254 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It delivers 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs operating at a base clock speed of 2.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 RAM running at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a 960 GB SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU is included in this configuration.

Benchmark results indicate strong processing and memory capabilities. The AMD EPYC processor achieves top-tier performance in stress-ng single-core CPU tests and bzip3 single-threaded decompression, placing in the top 10% of tested hardware. Multi-core performance is also strong, ranking in the top 25% for both stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests. The DDR5 memory subsystem demonstrates exceptional write bandwidth for large blocks and a top-tier PassMark memory score, while read bandwidth remains strong across both small and large blocks.

Given its bare metal architecture and high memory density of 8 GB per core, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for resource-intensive workloads that require dedicated hardware without virtualization overhead. The combination of 48 dedicated cores, high-speed DDR5 memory, and strong database operation benchmarks makes it highly suitable for memory-intensive databases, Redis deployments, multi-threaded data decompression, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: Vultr vbm-24c-384gb-amd is a bare metal server powered by an AMD EPYC 9254 processor with 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 2.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 384.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 6 GHz, a 960 GB local SSD, and no GPU. Performance benchmarks place this server in the top tier for single-core CPU execution, memory write bandwidth, and PassMark memory scores, with strong multi-core and database processing capabilities. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated bare-metal resources make it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads. It is highly suited for database operations, Redis caching, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.

tagline: A dedicated bare metal server combining 48 AMD EPYC cores with high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding database and memory-intensive workloads.
